meaning: To mention or introduce a topic.
hindiMeaning: किसी विषय पर चर्चा शुरू करना

examples.english: Don't bring up the past during the meeting.
examples.hindi: मीटिंग के दौरान अतीत की बात मत छेड़ना।

context: Used in conversations when someone starts talking about a specific topic.

meaning: To cancel an event or agreement.

examples.english: They had to call off the wedding due to bad weather.
examples.hindi: खराब मौसम के कारण उन्हें शादी रद्द करनी पड़ी।

context: Used for plans, meetings, or events that are no longer happening.

meaning: To continue doing something.

examples.english: Please carry on with your work, don't let me interrupt.
examples.hindi: कृपया अपना काम जारी रखें, मुझे बीच में टोकने न दें।

context: Used to encourage someone to continue their current activity.

meaning: To find or meet by chance.

examples.english: I came across my old diary while cleaning the room.
examples.hindi: कमरा साफ करते समय मुझे अपनी पुरानी डायरी अचानक मिल गई।

context: Used when you find an object or meet a person without planning.

meaning: To think of an idea or plan.

examples.english: We need to come up with a better solution.
examples.hindi: हमें कोई बेहतर समाधान सोचना होगा।

context: Common in brainstorming or problem-solving situations.

meaning: To break into pieces or fail completely.
hindiMeaning: टूट कर बिखर जाना या विफल होना

examples.english: The plan fell apart at the last minute.
examples.hindi: योजना आखिरी मिनट में विफल हो गई।

context: Used for relationships, plans, or objects that are breaking down.

meaning: To understand or solve something.

examples.english: I can't figure out how to use this machine.
examples.hindi: मुझे समझ नहीं आ रहा कि इस मशीन का उपयोग कैसे करना है।

context: Used when trying to find an answer or solution to a problem.

meaning: To discover information.

examples.english: I need to find out what time the train leaves.
examples.hindi: मुझे पता लगाना होगा कि ट्रेन कितने बजे जाती है।

context: Used when seeking knowledge or uncovering facts.

meaning: To have a good relationship with someone.
hindiMeaning: अच्छे संबंध होना या घुलमिल कर रहना

examples.english: My sister and I get along very well.
examples.hindi: मेरी बहन और मेरे बीच बहुत अच्छे संबंध हैं।

context: Describes the quality of a relationship between people.

meaning: To escape punishment for doing something wrong.

examples.english: You won't get away with cheating on the exam.
examples.hindi: तुम परीक्षा में नकल करके बच नहीं पाओगे।

context: Used when someone does a bad deed but faces no consequences.

meaning: To recover from an illness, loss, or difficulty.

examples.english: It took him a long time to get over the flu.
examples.hindi: उसे फ्लू से ठीक होने में काफी समय लगा।

context: Used for emotional or physical recovery.

meaning: To stop trying or quit a habit.

examples.english: Don't give up, keep trying until you succeed.
examples.hindi: हार मत मानो, सफल होने तक प्रयास करते रहो।

context: Used when someone stops doing something because it is too hard.

meaning: To continue or happen.

examples.english: What is going on here?
examples.hindi: यहाँ क्या हो रहा है?

context: Can mean 'to continue an action' or 'to take place'.

meaning: To wait for a short time.
hindiMeaning: थोड़ी देर रुकना या इंतज़ार करना

examples.english: Hold on a second, let me grab my keys.
examples.hindi: एक सेकंड रुको, मैं अपनी चाबियां ले लूं।

context: Often used on the phone or to ask someone to wait briefly.

meaning: To move or progress at the same rate as others.
hindiMeaning: साथ-साथ चलना या गति बनाए रखना

examples.english: You're walking too fast, I can't keep up.
examples.hindi: तुम बहुत तेज़ चल रहे हो, मैं साथ नहीं चल पा रहा हूँ।

context: Used when matching someone's speed in walking, working, or learning.

meaning: To take care of someone or something.

examples.english: Can you look after my dog while I'm on vacation?
examples.hindi: क्या तुम मेरी छुट्टियों के दौरान मेरे कुत्ते की देखभाल कर सकते हो?

context: Used for pets, children, or responsibilities.

meaning: To anticipate something with pleasure.

examples.english: I am looking forward to seeing you again.
examples.hindi: मैं आपसे दोबारा मिलने का बेसब्री से इंतज़ार कर रहा हूँ।

context: Common in polite correspondence and expressing excitement for future events.

meaning: To search for information in a book, dictionary, or online.

examples.english: If you don't know the meaning, look it up in the dictionary.
examples.hindi: अगर तुम्हें अर्थ नहीं पता, तो डिक्शनरी में खोजो।

context: Used when checking facts, words, or details.

meaning: To invent a story, excuse, or lie; also to become friends again after an argument.

examples.english: He made up a crazy excuse for being late.
examples.hindi: उसने देर से आने का एक अजीब बहाना बनाया।

context: Can mean creating something false, or fixing a relationship.

meaning: To postpone or delay doing something.

examples.english: We had to put off the meeting until tomorrow.
examples.hindi: हमें मीटिंग कल तक के लिए टालनी पड़ी।

context: Used for changing the time of an event to a later date.

meaning: To tolerate or endure something unpleasant.

examples.english: I can't put up with this noise any longer.
examples.hindi: मैं अब यह शोर और बर्दाश्त नहीं कर सकता।

context: Used when dealing with annoying people, pain, or difficult situations.

meaning: To meet someone by chance; to collide with something.

examples.english: I ran into my old teacher at the grocery store.
examples.hindi: मैं ग्रोसरी स्टोर पर अपने पुराने टीचर से अचानक मिला।

context: Used for unexpected meetings or physical collisions.

meaning: To have nothing left of something.

examples.english: We ran out of milk, so I need to go to the store.
examples.hindi: हमारा दूध खत्म हो गया है, इसलिए मुझे दुकान जाना होगा।

context: Used for supplies, time, or patience that have been completely used up.

meaning: To arrange, establish, or assemble something.

examples.english: They are planning to set up a new business.
examples.hindi: वे एक नया बिजनेस स्थापित करने की योजना बना रहे हैं।

context: Used for companies, equipment, or organizing events.

meaning: To arrive or appear at a place.

examples.english: He didn't show up for the interview.
examples.hindi: वह इंटरव्यू के लिए नहीं आया।

context: Usually used when arriving at a planned meeting or gathering.

meaning: To look or behave like an older family member.

examples.english: She really takes after her mother.
examples.hindi: वह बिल्कुल अपनी माँ पर गई है।

context: Used exclusively for comparing relatives in appearance or character.

meaning: To leave the ground (plane) or remove clothing.

examples.english: The plane will take off in ten minutes.
examples.hindi: विमान दस मिनट में उड़ान भरेगा।

context: Can refer to flights, fast success, or removing items of clothing.

meaning: To take control of something.

examples.english: He will take over as the new manager next week.
examples.hindi: वह अगले हफ्ते नए मैनेजर के रूप में ज़िम्मेदारी लेगा।

context: Used in business or situations where leadership is transferred.

meaning: To refuse or reject an offer or request; to reduce the volume.
hindiMeaning: अस्वीकार करना या आवाज़ कम करना

examples.english: I had to turn down the job offer.
examples.hindi: मुझे नौकरी का प्रस्ताव ठुकराना पड़ा।

context: Used for declining proposals or lowering sound/heat.

meaning: To result or end up in a particular way.

examples.english: The cake turned out really well.
examples.hindi: केक बहुत अच्छा बना (नतीजा अच्छा निकला)।

context: Used to describe the final outcome of an event or process.

meaning: To appear unexpectedly; to increase volume.
hindiMeaning: अचानक आ जाना या आवाज़ बढ़ाना

examples.english: My lost keys turned up in the sofa cushions.
examples.hindi: मेरी खोई हुई चाबियां सोफे के कुशन में मिल गईं।

context: Used for lost items being found, unexpected arrivals, or volume control.

meaning: To exercise; to solve a problem; to have a good result.
hindiMeaning: कसरत करना, हल निकालना या सफल होना

examples.english: I work out at the gym three times a week.
examples.hindi: मैं हफ्ते में तीन बार जिम में कसरत करता हूँ।

context: Can mean physical training or finding a solution to a difficult situation.

meaning: To stop working (machinery); to lose control of emotions.

examples.english: Our car broke down on the highway.
examples.hindi: हमारी कार हाईवे पर खराब हो गई।

context: Used for vehicles, equipment, or mental states.

meaning: To enter a building by force; to interrupt.
hindiMeaning: ज़बरदस्ती घुसना या बीच में टोकना

examples.english: Thieves tried to break in last night.
examples.hindi: कल रात चोरों ने ज़बरदस्ती घुसने की कोशिश की।

context: Commonly relates to crimes or interrupting a conversation.

meaning: To end a romantic relationship; to separate into pieces.

examples.english: They decided to break up after three years of dating.
examples.hindi: उन्होंने तीन साल डेटिंग के बाद रिश्ता खत्म करने का फैसला किया।

context: Most often used for couples ending their relationship.

meaning: To return a phone call.

examples.english: I'm busy right now, can I call you back?
examples.hindi: मैं अभी व्यस्त हूँ, क्या मैं तुम्हें वापस फोन कर सकता हूँ?

context: Standard telephone communication.

meaning: To become relaxed after being angry or excited.

examples.english: Take a deep breath and calm down.
examples.hindi: गहरी सांस लो और शांत हो जाओ।

context: Used to soothe someone who is angry, nervous, or panicked.

meaning: To reach the same point as someone else; to exchange news.
hindiMeaning: बराबरी पर आना या हालचाल लेना

examples.english: Let's grab a coffee and catch up.
examples.hindi: चलो कॉफ़ी पीते हैं और हालचाल लेते हैं।

context: Used in races, schoolwork, or socializing after a long time.

meaning: To register upon arrival (at a hotel, airport, etc.).
hindiMeaning: होटल या एयरपोर्ट आदि पर पहुंच दर्ज कराना

examples.english: We need to check in two hours before the flight.
examples.hindi: हमें उड़ान से दो घंटे पहले पहुंच दर्ज करानी होगी।

context: Travel-related term for arriving and confirming reservations.

meaning: To pay and leave a hotel; to look at or investigate something.

examples.english: Check out this cool new app I downloaded.
examples.hindi: यह बढ़िया नया ऐप देखो जो मैंने डाउनलोड किया है।

context: Used for departing from lodging or directing someone's attention to something interesting.

meaning: To become happier or to make someone happier.

examples.english: I brought you some flowers to cheer you up.
examples.hindi: मैं तुम्हें खुश करने के लिए कुछ फूल लाया हूँ।

context: Used when someone is feeling sad and needs a mood boost.

meaning: To make a place completely clean and tidy.

examples.english: Please clean up your room before guests arrive.
examples.hindi: मेहमानों के आने से पहले कृपया अपना कमरा साफ कर लें।

context: Used regarding chores or fixing a mess.

meaning: To separate into pieces.
hindiMeaning: अलग होना या टुकड़ों में टूटना

examples.english: The old book started to come apart in my hands.
examples.hindi: पुरानी किताब मेरे हाथों में ही अलग होने लगी।

context: Describing physical objects that are fragile or badly made.

meaning: To become sick with an illness.

examples.english: I think I'm coming down with a cold.
examples.hindi: मुझे लगता है कि मुझे जुखाम हो रहा है।

context: Used right as someone starts feeling the symptoms of a minor illness.

meaning: To rely or depend on someone or something.

examples.english: You can always count on me for help.
examples.hindi: तुम मदद के लिए हमेशा मुझ पर भरोसा कर सकते हो।

context: Expressing trust or reliability in a person or thing.

meaning: To consume less of something.
hindiMeaning: किसी चीज़ की मात्रा कम करना (जैसे खाना/पीना)

examples.english: I need to cut down on sugar to stay healthy.
examples.hindi: स्वस्थ रहने के लिए मुझे चीनी का सेवन कम करना होगा।

context: Common in health, diet, or spending habits.

meaning: To completely remove, disconnect, or isolate.
hindiMeaning: काट देना या अलग कर देना

examples.english: The phone company cut off our service.
examples.hindi: फोन कंपनी ने हमारी सेवा काट दी।

context: Used for stopping the supply of utilities, isolating places, or interrupting.

meaning: To visit someone informally for a short time.
hindiMeaning: थोड़ी देर के लिए मिलने आना

examples.english: Feel free to drop by my office anytime.
examples.hindi: मेरे ऑफिस में कभी भी आने के लिए बेझिझक रहें।

context: A casual invitation or spontaneous visit.

meaning: To take someone or something to a place and leave it there; to fall asleep.
hindiMeaning: छोड़ना (व्यक्ति या सामान) या नींद आ जाना

examples.english: I need to drop off these packages at the post office.
examples.hindi: मुझे ये पैकेज पोस्ट ऑफिस में छोड़ने हैं।

context: Very common in driving, errands, or getting tired.

meaning: To quit a class, school, or program.
hindiMeaning: पढ़ाई या कोर्स बीच में छोड़ देना

examples.english: He had to drop out of college due to financial reasons.
examples.hindi: आर्थिक कारणों से उसे कॉलेज छोड़ना पड़ा।

context: Used in the context of education or competitions.

meaning: To finally be in a particular place, state, or situation.
hindiMeaning: अंत में किसी स्थिति या स्थान पर पहुंचना

examples.english: If you don't study, you will end up failing.
examples.hindi: यदि तुम नहीं पढ़ोगे, तो अंत में तुम फेल हो जाओगे।

context: Used to describe an unplanned or eventual outcome.

meaning: To move slower than others; to fail to keep a schedule.

examples.english: She fell behind in her studies after being sick.
examples.hindi: बीमार होने के बाद वह अपनी पढ़ाई में पिछड़ गई।

context: Used in academics, races, or paying bills late.

meaning: To add information to empty spaces (like a form); to substitute for someone.
hindiMeaning: खाली स्थान भरना या किसी की जगह काम करना

examples.english: Can you fill in for me at work tomorrow?
examples.hindi: क्या तुम कल काम पर मेरी जगह काम कर सकते हो?

context: Used with forms/blanks or replacing a worker temporarily.

meaning: To complete a form or document with requested information.

examples.english: Please fill out this application form.
examples.hindi: कृपया यह आवेदन फॉर्म भरें।

context: Almost exclusively used with paperwork and documents.

meaning: To survive or manage with minimum resources (usually money).

examples.english: It's hard to get by on such a low salary.
examples.hindi: इतने कम वेतन पर गुज़ारा करना मुश्किल है।

context: Relates to living on a tight budget or just barely passing/managing.

meaning: To enter a place, car, or building; to arrive.

examples.english: What time did you get in last night?
examples.hindi: कल रात तुम कितने बजे पहुँचे (घर आये)?

context: Used for small vehicles (cars/taxis) or arriving home/work.

meaning: To leave a bus, train, plane, or to finish work.
hindiMeaning: उतरना (वाहन से) या काम से छुट्टी पाना

examples.english: We need to get off at the next stop.
examples.hindi: हमें अगले स्टॉप पर उतरना होगा।

context: Used for large public transport or ending a work shift.

meaning: To avoid doing something you don't want to do.

examples.english: He tried to get out of doing the dishes.
examples.hindi: उसने बर्तन धोने के काम से बचने की कोशिश की।

context: Used when shirking responsibilities or chores.

meaning: To give something for free; to reveal a secret.
hindiMeaning: मुफ्त में देना या राज़ खोलना

examples.english: Please don't give away the ending of the movie.
examples.hindi: कृपया फिल्म का अंत (राज़) मत बताना।

context: Used for donations or accidentally exposing surprises/secrets.

meaning: To finally agree to what someone wants after refusing; to surrender.

examples.english: The children kept begging for ice cream until their mother gave in.
examples.hindi: बच्चे आइसक्रीम के लिए तब तक मिन्नतें करते रहे जब तक उनकी माँ मान नहीं गईं।

context: Used in negotiations, parenting, or yielding to pressure.

meaning: To oppose or disagree with something/someone.

examples.english: I can't go against my parents' wishes.
examples.hindi: मैं अपने माता-पिता की इच्छा के खिलाफ नहीं जा सकता।

context: Used when breaking rules, expectations, or confronting authorities.

meaning: To review, examine, or check something carefully.

examples.english: Let's go over the plan one more time.
examples.hindi: चलो एक बार फिर से योजना की समीक्षा करते हैं।

context: Common in business, studying, or double-checking details.

meaning: To develop into an adult; to behave maturely.

examples.english: He grew up in a small village.
examples.hindi: वह एक छोटे से गाँव में बड़ा हुआ।

context: Talks about childhood backgrounds or telling someone to stop being childish.

meaning: To submit homework, a report, or resignation to an authority.

examples.english: I have to hand in my essay by tomorrow.
examples.hindi: मुझे कल तक अपना निबंध जमा करना है।

context: Used primarily in school or workplace environments.

meaning: To distribute something to a group of people.

examples.english: The teacher will hand out the worksheets now.
examples.hindi: शिक्षक अब वर्कशीट बांटेंगे।

context: Used for giving flyers, papers, or items to multiple people.

meaning: To spend time casually without a specific purpose.

examples.english: Do you want to hang out at the mall this weekend?
examples.hindi: क्या तुम इस वीकेंड मॉल में घूमना-फिरना चाहते हो?

context: Very common informal phrasing for friends spending time together.

meaning: To end a phone call.

examples.english: Don't hang up on me, I'm still talking!
examples.hindi: मेरा फोन मत काटना, मैं अभी भी बात कर रहा हूँ!

context: Specifically related to telephone conversations.

meaning: To restrain or not show emotion; to delay progress.

examples.english: She tried to hold back her tears.
examples.hindi: उसने अपने आँसू रोकने की कोशिश की।

context: Used regarding emotions, progress, or physical restraint.

meaning: To continue doing something, often despite difficulty.

examples.english: Just keep on walking until you see the sign.
examples.hindi: बस तब तक चलते रहो जब तक कि तुम्हें साइन (बोर्ड) न दिख जाए।

context: Encouraging continuous action.

meaning: To disappoint someone by failing to meet expectations.

examples.english: I trusted you, please don't let me down.
examples.hindi: मैंने तुम पर भरोसा किया, कृपया मुझे निराश मत करना।

context: Used for emotional disappointment or broken promises.

meaning: To allow someone or something to enter.

examples.english: Open the window and let some fresh air in.
examples.hindi: खिड़की खोलो और ताजी हवा को अंदर आने दो।

context: Used for doors, windows, and giving permission to enter.

meaning: To think about the past.
hindiMeaning: अतीत के बारे में सोचना या मुड़कर देखना

examples.english: When I look back on my childhood, I smile.
examples.hindi: जब मैं अपने बचपन के बारे में सोचता हूँ, तो मैं मुस्कुराता हूँ।

context: Used for nostalgia, memories, or reviewing past events.

meaning: To think that you are better than someone else.

examples.english: He looks down on people who have less money.
examples.hindi: वह उन लोगों को हीन समझता है जिनके पास कम पैसा है।

context: Shows arrogance or snobbery toward others.

meaning: To be careful or watch out for danger.

examples.english: Look out! There is a car coming.
examples.hindi: सावधान! एक कार आ रही है।

context: Used as a sudden warning command.

meaning: To examine or review something quickly.
hindiMeaning: सरसरी नज़र से देखना या जांचना

examples.english: Can you look over my resume before I send it?
examples.hindi: क्या तुम भेजने से पहले मेरा रेज़्यूमे जांच सकते हो?

context: Common for documents, essays, or contracts.

meaning: A polite way of saying 'to die'.

examples.english: His grandfather passed away last year.
examples.hindi: उनके दादाजी पिछले साल गुज़र गए।

context: Used as a respectful euphemism for death.

meaning: To return money owed to someone.

examples.english: I will pay you back on Friday.
examples.hindi: मैं शुक्रवार को तुम्हें पैसे वापस कर दूँगा।

context: Used in financial contexts involving loans among friends or banks.

meaning: To result in success or benefit; to finish paying a debt.

examples.english: All her hard work finally paid off.
examples.hindi: उसकी सारी कड़ी मेहनत आखिरकार सफल हुई।

context: Often refers to investments (time, effort, money) yielding good results.

meaning: To choose or select someone or something.

examples.english: She helped me pick out a nice dress.
examples.hindi: उसने मुझे एक अच्छी ड्रेस चुनने में मदद की।

context: Used for shopping, making decisions from a group, or identifying things.

meaning: To lift something; to collect someone; to learn something easily.
hindiMeaning: उठाना, ले जाना या जल्दी सीख लेना

examples.english: Can you pick me up from the airport?
examples.hindi: क्या तुम मुझे एयरपोर्ट से ले सकते हो?

context: Has many meanings, heavily used in driving contexts or acquiring skills/objects.
